title: Add multi-arch image build
body:
Add Github Action to do multi-arch build for 4 architectures(`amd64`,
`s390x`, `ppc64le`, `arm64`) from 2 sources - `contrib/buildahimage/stable` and
`contrib/buildahimage/upstream`.
The multi-arch image build is done via emulation (no real hardwarer is
required).
New images are built after each push to master branch and once in a day
on schedule.
- stable image is pushed to `quay.io/buildah/stable:v..` and `quay.io/containers/buildah:v..`
- upstream image is pushed to `quay.io/buildah/upstream:master`

`CONTAINERS_QUAY_USERNAME` and `CONTAINERS_QUAY_PASSWORD`, as well as
`BUILDAH_QUAY_USERNAME` and `BUILDAH_QUAY_PASSWORD` should be created in
advance.

Test run with push to another repos is available at https://github.com/barthy1/buildah/actions/runs/558348033

Signed-off-by: Yulia Gaponenko <yulia.gaponenko1@de.ibm.com>

comments.body:
@cevich Would you be so kind and create [secret env variables](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/encrypted-secrets#creating-encrypted-secrets-for-a-repository) to push images?
`CONTAINERS_QUAY_USERNAME`, `CONTAINERS_QUAY_PASSWORD`, `BUILDAH_QUAY_USERNAME`, `BUILDAH_QUAY_PASSWORD`.

> Would you be so kind and create secret env variables to push images?

Yep, happy to.

> Would you be so kind and create secret env variables to push images?

Okay, this is done.  With the assumption that you intended the `quay.io/containers/buildah` image.

> Okay, this is done. With the assumption that you intended the `quay.io/containers/buildah` image.

Thank you! Yeah, testing image push with other quay repos + having buildah as org name and repo name in different cases confused me :( I will double check all container image names.

> I will double check all container image names.

Yeah...not really an easy way to test this out live :confused: 

It would be a pain to setup, but we do have `github.com/containers/automation_sandbox`.  I could fork buildah over there, copy the secrets, and copy this code to see if it works properly on a master branch.

Another idea is...add a temporary commit here to activate the workfow for `on: pull_request`, maybe using a forced `testing` tag on the images.  Either way, it would be convenient to find runtime problems this way rather than after merging.

We need to be a little careful merging this PR anyway, to ensure it doesn't conflict with the cron-jobs @TomSweeneyRedHat has running (to push to these same image names).
@cevich what do you think just about separate temporary branch in this repo? PR can be merged to it and tested properly.

>  just about separate temporary branch in this repo

That's an excellent idea actually, thanks for the suggestion.  I'll look into making this happen...
...okay, permission-granted. I'll get this PR's code over onto a test branch, so we can see it run (with a test tag)...
...ugh, sorry I didn't manage to get to this today like I hoped.  It's on my list for first thing tomorrow morning, along with my coffee :smile:
LGTM, but my yaml knowledge is extremely limited.....

@barthy1 okay, I created a testing branch with your commits + 1 that adds a "testing_" prefix to the tags: https://github.com/containers/buildah/tree/temp_automation_test

Unf. github-actions doesn't appear to want to run anything on that branch.  I'll try to investigate "why".

>  Unf. github-actions doesn't appear to want to run anything on that branch. I'll try to investigate "why".

you need to put correct branch name - temp_automation_test here https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/temp_automation_test/.github/workflows/multi-arch-build.yaml#L5
Good catch, that did the trick: https://github.com/containers/buildah/actions/workflows/multi-arch-build.yaml

Cool, it seems to be working (ignoring the `testing_` prefix I added):

* [pushing manifest for quay.io/buildah/stable:testing_v1.18.0](https://github.com/containers/buildah/runs/1928674625?check_suite_focus=true#step:10:169
)
* [pushing manifest for quay.io/buildah/upstream:testing_master](https://github.com/containers/buildah/runs/1928674551?check_suite_focus=true#step:10:168)
* [pushing manifest for quay.io/containers/buildah:testing_v1.18.0](https://github.com/containers/buildah/runs/1928674625?check_suite_focus=true#step:12:172)

I'm able to inspect the images for the various architectures with command like:
`skopeo --override-arch=ppc64le inspect docker://quay.io/buildah/stable:testing_v1.18.0
| jq .Architecture`
Okay, this LGTM then (but it needs a rebase).

Run on native hardware:
```
# docker run quay.io/containers/buildah:testing_v1.18.0 buildah version
Version:         1.18.0
Go Version:      go1.15.2
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:
image Version:   5.8.0
Git Commit:
Built:           Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/s390x
```
```
docker run quay.io/buildah/upstream:testing_master buildah version
Version:         1.20.0-dev
Go Version:      go1.15.8
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  v0.8.1
image Version:   5.10.2
Git Commit:      a6eeca7a
Built:           Thu Feb 18 16:54:01 2021
OS/Arch:         linux/ppc64le
```

Hmmm, Cirrus-CI seems to have gotten "stuck".  I canceled the wedged tasks and restarted them.  The GitHub status should update in 45m or so (when the re-runs complete).  Until then, it will continue showing "X" due to a github bug.
@barthy1 I see some merge commits made it into the PR, was that intentional?  Maybe you intended to rebase instead?

@cevich when branch is out-of-date in buildah repo, I can see the "Update branch" button at the bottom of my PR, which actually merges master to the branch. I thought it's allowed way to keep branch up-to-date, taking into account how frequent branch becomes out-of-date.. It seems I was wrong and I will rebase the branch.

@barthy1 Is this ready to go in?

@cevich @edsantiago @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL
@rhatdan yes, the code is ready from my point of view

Yes, @rhatdan I believe this is good to go, I [tested it on a temporary branch and verified it successfully builds and pushes images.](https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3006#issuecomment-781587296).  There are a few scripting nit's but nothing worth holding up this PR.

/approve
/lgtm

* [quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.18.0](https://github.com/containers/buildah/runs/1971494316?check_suite_focus=true#step:10:170)
* [quay.io/containers/buildah:v1.18.0](https://github.com/containers/buildah/runs/1971494316?check_suite_focus=true#step:12:169)
